skill.name: slide-deck-images
skill.description: Generate professional slide-deck SVG images (not PPTX/PDF) when users ask to create slides, slide decks, or presentations and need image outputs.
skill.summary: Generates styled SVG slide images with a structured workflow, multiple visual presets, and selective regeneration support.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 460 static analysis findings are false positives. The 178 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are triggered by hex color codes (#FFFFFF, #1A1A1A) in slide style definitions. The 278 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples in documentation files. The 'C2 keywords', 'System reconnaissance', 'Hardcoded URL', and 'Hidden file access' findings are all documentation references to design terms, standard file paths, and the SVG XML namespace. The skill is a legitimate slide deck image generator with a Python script that reads prompt files and writes SVG output. It makes no network calls and contains no cryptographic operations. The skill invokes 'python scripts/generate_svg.py' locally and writes files to a 'slides-svg/' directory, which are documented and expected behaviors.

low_findings.title: Unrecognized tool reference in prompt template
low_findings.description: The base-prompt.md template references 'nano banana pro' as an image generation tool. This appears to be a non-existent placeholder name. It is not a security threat but indicates unclear instructions in the prompt template.

content.user_title: Create Professional Slide Deck Images with SVG
content.value_statement: Users often need slide images for sharing and reading, but standard tools focus on PPTX or PDF output. This skill generates SVG slide images with professional design, multiple style presets, and a structured workflow for outline review, prompt generation, and image rendering.
actual_capabilities: Generates SVG slide images from text content with professional templates
Applies visual presets including corporate, minimal, blueprint, chalkboard, sketch-notes, and 10 more styles
Supports custom style dimensions for texture, mood, typography, and density
Provides structured workflow with optional outline and prompt review steps
Enables selective slide regeneration without rebuilding the full deck
Adapts to multiple languages and audience types with content analysis
limitations: Output is SVG image files only, not PPTX or PDF format
Requires Python 3.10 or higher for local SVG rendering
Each slide is a standalone image without animation or transition effects
Cannot edit existing image files or import from external formats

use_cases.title: Executive creates investor presentation
use_cases.description: A business leader needs a professional slide deck for an investor meeting. The skill generates corporate-styled SVG slides with clear metrics, structured layouts, and consistent branding, outputting shareable image files.

use_cases.title: Educator prepares teaching materials
use_cases.description: A teacher needs visual aids for a classroom lesson. Using the chalkboard or sketch-notes preset, the skill creates engaging educational slides with hand-drawn aesthetics. The outline review step helps organize topics.

use_cases.title: Researcher presents scientific findings
use_cases.description: A researcher needs a clean, technical slide deck for a conference. The scientific preset creates dense, data-friendly slides. Selective regeneration lets the researcher update specific slides without regenerating the full deck.

prompt_templates.title: Quick slide deck creation
prompt_templates.prompt: /slide-deck "Explain our Q4 growth strategy to the board of directors"
prompt_templates.scenario: Creates a complete slide deck from a single prompt using default style.

prompt_templates.title: Deck with specific style preset
prompt_templates.scenario: Creates slides with a hand-drawn sketch-notes style preset for educational content.

prompt_templates.title: Outline-only preview
prompt_templates.scenario: Generates only the slide outline for review before committing to full generation.

prompt_templates.title: Selective slide regeneration
prompt_templates.scenario: Regenerates only slides 3 and 7 while keeping all other slides unchanged.

output_examples.input: Generate a 3-slide corporate deck on market trends
output: Slide 1: Cover slide with title "Market Trends 2026" and subtitle overlay on brand-colored background
Slide 2: Content slide with 4 bullet points on key growth areas and a data highlight box
Slide 3: Closing slide with strategic recommendations and a clear call-to-action

output_examples.input: Create an educational deck on photosynthesis with sketch style
output: Slide 1: Cover with hand-drawn leaf illustration and large title in chalk-like lettering
Slide 2: Diagram slide showing the photosynthesis process with labeled arrows and annotations
Slide 3: Summary slide with key takeaways in bullet format and a fun fact callout

best_practices: Specify the target audience with --audience to get appropriate content density and visual treatment
Use --outline-only to review and refine the slide structure before generating full images
Select a style preset that matches your content type for the best visual results
anti_patterns: Requesting image formats other than SVG will not work. The skill generates SVG images only, not PNG, JPG, PPTX, or PDF.
Avoid very long single prompts with multiple unrelated topics. Break complex subjects into separate deck requests.
Do not skip the outline review step for important presentations. The review step helps catch structural issues early.

faq.question: What image format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: This skill generates SVG image files only. It does not create PPTX, PDF, PNG, or JPG files.

faq.question: Does this skill require any software installation?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires Python 3.10 or higher to run the SVG generation script locally.

faq.question: What style presets are available?
faq.answer: There are 16 presets including blueprint, corporate, minimal, chalkboard, sketch-notes, scientific, vintage, and more. Each preset combines texture, mood, typography, and density settings.

faq.question: Can I update only specific slides without regenerating everything?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --regenerate flag followed by slide numbers to re-render only selected slides.

faq.question: Can this skill edit my existing slide images?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates new slides from text content. It cannot edit existing image files.

faq.question: Does this skill work with languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --lang flag to specify a language, or set it to auto for automatic detection from input content.
